configurations.name: with_skill
configurations.description: Response with relevant skill SKILL.md loaded into context

configurations.name: without_skill
configurations.description: Response using only base model knowledge

analyst_observations: Assertion pass rates are 100% for both configurations — the automated assertions are non-discriminating at this threshold. The assertions catch the presence of key concepts but cannot measure depth, precision, or safety of the advice. Qualitative scoring reveals a meaningful +0.69 point gap that assertions miss.

Skills add the most value on multi-domain intersection questions (rebalancing + tax-loss harvesting: +1.25 delta) where the skill provides structured checklists and cross-domain coordination that base knowledge lacks.
Skills add meaningful value on compliance nuance (fiduciary: +1.0 delta) through frameworks like the mitigation hierarchy and the disclosure-vs-consent distinction.

Skills add the least value when base model knowledge is already strong and the question is well-represented in training data (Reg BI rollover: -0.25 delta). The without_skill response actually covered an edge case (NUA) that the skill missed.
Token overhead averages 40% for with-skill runs (reading SKILL.md files adds context). Duration overhead is only 10%, suggesting the extra tokens are mostly input (skill content) not output.

Recommendation: Assertions need to be more discriminating — add depth-of-analysis assertions (e.g., 'provides 3+ specific cross-account wash-sale sources') and safety assertions (e.g., 'warns about NIIT threshold') to capture the qualitative gap.
